name: Howard Alden
abstract: Howard Vincent Alden (born October 17, 1958) is an American jazz guitarist born in Newport Beach, California. Alden has recorded many albums for Concord Records, including four with seven-string guitar innovator George Van Eps.

interviews.title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: Howard and I love playing together. We’ve had a long musical relationship and we’ve done some duo records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Tate
about_quotes.evidence: The first night that the Alden-Barrett quintet worked... Howard Alden... I’m hired and I go out and I work private parties with Howard Alden... We recorded it in Belfast in 1996 with Dave McKenna, Butch Miles, Harry Allen and Howard Alden
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Tate interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: So then I had Howard Alden and there's twenty two; thirty two was Anat,; Howard was forty nine... And that was the band I had. And we had like a ten minute rehearsal, played for a week, two shows a night, and had the ball of our lives.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: This one was mostly Howard, Howard Alden. He did a marvelous job. And mainly these things were pre-recorded and Howard taught Sean Penn to mime his playing very well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: In fact we’re going to do it tonight, the Breezin’ Along group. I’ll have fun with that. Because Howard Alden can read my mind
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: the band on that record was Dick Katz, Howard Alden, Phil Flanigan, Mel Lewis, Bobby Pring, Eddie Bert and Matt Finders
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
